refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall generate and maintain a high-fidelity digital twin of the worksite, integrating terrain geometry, static/dynamic obstacles, and geofenced work-zone boundaries.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was broad; refinement specifies the 'digital twin' approach to ensure data consistency across planning and safety functions, addressing the high safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate real-time map updates in a simulated environment with 99% accuracy of obstacle detection and <500ms latency for zone updates.
sub_frs: Capture terrain and obstacle data via onboard and offboard sensors.
Synthesize sensor data into a unified spatial model.
Distribute the worksite model to planning and safety services.

potential_sources: Latency between Vehicle sensors and Centralized Engine
coupling_check.notes: Decouples individual vehicle processing from the global site state, ensuring a single source of truth for safety zones.

assumptions_risks: Risk of stale data if network latency spikes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Incorporate a local 'safety-buffer' cache on vehicles to mitigate temporary network loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system orchestrator responsible for cross-module data synthesis and global state management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingestion of raw sensor and position data from vehicles.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transport layer for all site-wide data distribution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Triggering alerts based on work-zone violations detected in the model.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary owner of the global model; low diffusion as it centralizes the logic.
Vehicles provide data but should not own the global site-wide synthesis logic.
Consumer of the map, not a producer/maintainer.
High overlap with safety zone logic, but lacks terrain modeling capabilities.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the modeling engine within the NGHE Solution module minimizes interface complexity for the Site Manager and Safety Manager.
containment_risks: Single point of failure for site-wide safety logic
Potential bottleneck for real-time vehicle updates
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear fail-safe behaviors for the Vehicle module when the NGHE Solution model is unreachable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The Centralized Engine DP directly satisfies the requirement for a unified work-zone and terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Logic for obstacle detection might leak into the Vehicle module if not strictly defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate container for global state.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; centralized data is easier to audit and verify against site surveys.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation to the system-level orchestrator.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define the 'Safety Services' interface for zone violation handoffs.

decision.justification: The high-level mapping strategy is sound and assigned to the correct module. Decomposition to the next level is required to define specific sub-functions for data ingestion, processing, and distribution.
next_level_sub_frs: Ingest multi-modal sensor streams via Telemetry.
Execute geometric point-cloud registration for terrain updates.
Validate dynamic obstacle vectors against safety geofences.

revise.why: Need to specify the internal architecture of the modeling engine (e.g., microservices vs. monolithic) in the next level.

assumptions: Vehicle sensors provide sufficient resolution for obstacle classification.
open_risks: Computational load of real-time 3D modeling at scale.
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy specifications from Vehicle vendors
